steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question. The passage directly states that Hooks and Ward "had met while performing in a 1960 touring production of Lorraine Hansberry's play A Raisin in the Sun." That single sentence is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Hooks and Ward participated in the same workshop at NEC.': The passage never mentions a workshop introducing them; NEC was founded after they met. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Hooks and Ward attended the same performance of The Great MacDaddy.': The Great MacDaddy premiered in 1974, more than a decade after they met. The passage does not link their first meeting to that play. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Harrison introduced Hooks to Ward at a performance of The Great MacDaddy.': The passage says nothing about Harrison making an introduction; this fabricates a detail not in the text. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Hooks and Ward were both actors in a production of A Raisin in the Sun.': The passage explicitly says they "met while performing in a 1960 touring production" of that play, confirming both were performers in it. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
